steps.text: Initiate dialogue through written correspondence announcing a regulatory action that may have Tribal implications.
Send copies of correspondence relating to NEPA or NHPA actions to the Tribal Historic Preservation Officer (THPO) or equivalent staff.
Describe the proposed regulatory action and identify the location and methods for the Tribe to provide comments or request additional information.
Include information on the environmental review and NHPA Section 106 processes and an invitation for participation.
Conduct a follow-up meeting with Tribal officials or representatives based on expressions of interest from individual Tribes.
Demonstrate the importance NRC places on government to government relationships with Tribal nations.
Avoid judgmental attitudes.
Be flexible in conducting meetings and accept different views on decision making.
Keep your voice at a moderate or reasonable level.
Practice patience when speaking with and listening to Tribal elders and other Tribal representatives.
Give undivided attention to the person who is speaking.
Avoid rude behavior while a Tribal elder is talking.
Consider cultural norms regarding eye contact.
Avoid inappropriate laughter during discussions with Tribal elders or officials.
Dress respectfully as for any business meeting with elected officials or distinguished representatives of a government.
Consider the prior experience, involvement, education, and training of the Tribe as related to the technical or licensing matter at hand.
Determine the need to provide explanations for technical and licensing terms.
Respect Tribal interests, cultural diversity, customs, and agendas.
Respect Tribal Council leaders as officials of another government.
Listen carefully; ask questions for clarification.
Consider what you say; do not promise what you may be unable to deliver.
Communicate with the Tribe because it is the key to successful relationships.
Familiarize yourself with the Tribe’s specific information and understand the history and current issues, concerns, and expertise of the Tribe or Tribes with whom you may work.
